theme_summary: Weak cave goblins, scavenging vermin, damp-hollow pests, and a hidden feeding horror squatting in a shallow stone hollow under low torchlight and mushroom baskets.

theme_summary: Weak boglings and marsh scavengers nesting around a dark green mire cave with reeds, mud, fungus baskets, and shallow blue-lit puddles.
background_prompt_shared: Grounded medieval fantasy mire cave and marsh hollow, full-body enemy scene background, same family zone for every monster, dark green wet stone, shallow black-green water, reed clusters, mud banks, root tangles, fungus baskets, driftwood stakes, slime-slick cave walls, dim overcast marsh light filtering in from outside, mostly dark green and algae-olive ambience with only small pale blue accents in damp membranes, fungus growths, and puddle reflections, no crowd, no heroic framing, clearly low-tier and primitive; vary the exact composition per monster within this same mire hollow by placing them near different landmarks such as reed mats, puddles, fungus baskets, root arches, mud shelves, or narrow water channels.
family_flavor_text: Keeps more mud than shelter and more hunger than wit. Even so, the mire has shaped them into something hard to catch and harder to forget.

theme_summary: Kiln-yard drudges, ash-fed scavenger beasts, and weak kiln-born refuse things turned territorial among dead furnaces and lime-choked rubble.
